steps.title: Identify the implication
steps.content: The model uses a uniform, prescribed methane mole fraction at the lowest level to handle data sparsity. But the methane mole fraction influences precipitation and humidity. So fixing it artificially means the model's outputs for those phenomena will not match reality perfectly.

steps.title: Match to a choice
steps.content:
Choice A states this directly: "some disagreements between the model's simulations... and the moon's actual precipitation and humidity are to be expected."
  • B asserts a specific direction not supported
  • C is a future hope unrelated to the current implication
  • D blames "variations" but the model uses a uniform value
, contradicting the premise.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
